name: PAPILLARY ADENOMA OF BILE DUCTS
definition: very rare benign tumor of biliary tract

demographics.Path: usually solitary tumor / biliary papillomatosis with papillary fronds extending into
demographics.Histo: columnar epithelium supported by connective tissue from lamina propria ± mucin
demographics.Location: common bile duct > right / left hepatic duct

distribution: common bile duct > right / left hepatic duct
discriminating_features: visualized at cross-sectional imaging only if large enough

general: visualized at cross-sectional imaging only if large enough

clinical.Prognosis: high rate of recurrence after surgical resection
clinical.Cx: malignant transformation (rare in solitary adenoma, significant risk in papillomatosis))
